notes: Scope: commit 87e5a61 — pure JSON plumbing of llmSupport{Image,Audio,Thinking} + defaultConfig.systemPromptDefault from the shipped model_allowlist.json asset through Gson into AllowedModel/AllowedModelConfig and onward into Model.configs via createLlmChatConfigs. No network, IPC, permissions, or user-input paths are introduced. Audit verdict: pass — no critical, major, or minor security findings in this change. Key observations: (1) Trust boundary — model_allowlist.json is a signed APK asset, not an attacker-controllable input, so systemPromptDefault content is developer-controlled; APK signature verification at install time enforces integrity (A08). (2) A03 injection — the string is only stored in Model.configValues here; wiring into engine.createConversation(systemInstruction=...) is explicitly deferred to task 10. Even once wired, the sink is a local on-device LLM, not an SQL/OS/LDAP/shell interpreter; LLM system-instruction by definition accepts arbitrary text, so there is no injection primitive in this commit. (3) A04 insecure design — missing-field handling is fail-closed and correct: llmSupport* default to false (Kotlin default param), systemPromptDefault handled via nullable chain + .orEmpty() so both missing defaultConfig and explicit JSON null collapse to "". (4) A08 data integrity — Gson is used without enableComplexMapKeySerialization/enableDefaultTyping/polymorphic type resolvers, so no insecure-deserialization vector is introduced; AllowlistLoader.parse schema-guard regexes for modelId/modelFile/commitHash/sizeInBytes/URL-prefix remain intact and were not weakened. (5) A02/secrets — no hardcoded keys, tokens, passwords, or credentials in the diff. (6) Tests in AllowlistLoaderTest cover true/false/missing permutations for all four new fields, including the NPE-safe default path — good defense-in-depth for the A04 missing-field class of bug. Forward-looking, non-blocking note (explicitly out of task-1 scope, no finding filed): if a future task ever loads the allowlist from an untrusted channel (remote fetch, user JSON import), it would be worth adding a systemPromptDefault length cap (e.g. 8KB) + control-char filter to harden against DoS-by-giant-string and LLM prompt-smuggling via embedded role tokens like <|system|>. Current shipped-asset model makes that unnecessary.
